attacking_style: Not applicable - BMC Racing Team is a squad, not an individual rider. Analysis focused on team time trial dominance through specialized equipment, preparation, and tactical execution.
team_tactics: Extensive pre-race reconnaissance (4+ course recons documented), winter-long R&D investment in TTT equipment, strategic use of powerhouse riders like Dennis for final surges to maximize time gaps.
terrain_preference: Team time trials across all distances (22-35km). Consistently elite performance regardless of course profile, suggesting depth and versatility rather than terrain specificity.
vulnerability: Data set limited to TTT victories only - no evidence of performance in mass-start scenarios, climbs, or individual efforts. Vulnerable when individual firepower required over collective discipline.
key_patterns: TTT victories by narrow margins (4 seconds twice), suggesting precision pacing over pure power dominance.
Multi-reconnaissance approach to course preparation standard operating procedure across Grand Tours and stage races.
Winter R&D investment cycles translate to summer Grand Tour TTT success (documented 2018).
Consistent marginal gains philosophy: technical preparation and equipment optimization prioritized over rider firepower alone.
form_trajectory: positive: Back-to-back TTT wins at Tirreno-Adriatico (2017-2018) building into Tour de France yellow jersey via TTT dominance. Institutional competence increasing.
